motivation: Modify class definitions using annotation hooks.
whyExists: Real-world systems model real-world entities. An e-commerce platform deals with Users, Products, Orders, Payments, and Shipments — each with distinct state and behavior. OOP maps these naturally to classes. Encapsulation protects data integrity. Inheritance eliminates duplication of shared behavior. Polymorphism allows new types to be added without modifying existing code (the Open/Closed Principle). These properties make large codebases maintainable, extensible, and testable.
realWorldAnalogy: Imagine you are working in a warehouse. Instead of tossing boxes around randomly, you place them into labeled shelves. When a package arrives, you look up its shelf label to update the stock index. Similarly, **Class Decorators** acts as a named workspace or rule that coordinates statements, keeping variables safe and ensuring operations execute only when matching conditions are met.
simpleExplanation: To learn Class Decorators from first principles, start by looking at how data resolves at runtime. Imagine your processor executing instructions sequentially. Class Decorators defines how values bind, operations evaluate, and stack structures are clean.
visualDiagram:
```text
[User Input] ──► [Process Scope: Class Decorators] ──► [Memory Address] ──► [Console Output]
```
syntaxExplanation: Verify that all brackets, colons, keywords, and assignments match the typescript syntax specifications. Avoid naming collisions by using scoped namespaces.

stepByStepExecution.desc: Allocate memory segment and load bindings.
